title: BJP govt. in West Bengalis fulfilling the promises made during polls: Shah
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Home Minister Amit Shah lauded the contribution of the Jana Sangh founder in nation building on his 125th birth anniversary

title: Seafarers to undergo mandatory yoga-based wellness training
content: Govt. requires all seafarers holding a valid Indian Continuous Discharge Certificate (CDC) to complete the course within one year, and mandates that all candidates at DGS-approved Maritime Training Institutes (MTIs) finish the modules

title: Madhya Pradesh govt. reconstitutes Waqf Board under new law, inclusion of Hindu members draws criticism
content: Congress MLA Arif Masood terms the move ‘completely wrong’ as the matter related to various provisions of the new Act is pending before the Supreme Court

title: Won’t allow govt control of Ayodhya Ram temple at any cost, says VHP
content: But it has no objection to appointing a professional Chief Executive Officer, provided the person is not a serving bureaucrat and is accountable to Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust

title: Form used in rehearsal for second phase of Census has an ‘open column’ to record caste
content: Final methodology to enumerate caste as part of Census 2027 will be prepared based on the feedback received during the test exercise; the open column methodology had resulted in the 2011 caste survey returning over 46 lakh different ‘caste names’

title: Modi begins three-nation trip, starts with Indonesia to boost strategic ties
content: Prime Minister set to hold discussions with Indonesian President Subianto to add ‘momentum to this partnership across diverse sectors’; he will travel to Australia and New Zealand next as part of efforts to promote a free and open Indo-Pacific

title: Bikers perform wheelies in front of ambulance, endanger patient’s life in Bengaluru
content: According to witnesses, the riders were not wearing helmets nor were number plates visible in the two-wheelers

title: Residents flag dangerous road conditions across East Bengaluru’s IT corridor
content: These roads serve major residential areas, IT corridor, schools and hospitals but are filled with potholes and pose a risk to motorists

title: Safe Footpath Campaign runs into conflict with street vendors in Bengaluru
content: Hundreds of street vendors have been evicted and the wares they were hawking seized, as part of the drive in the past one week.

title: MBA felicitates Justice V. Mohana, first woman from Tamil Nadu to be elevated directly to Supreme Court
content: She also inaugurates an endowment lecture series and hails the contributions of M.K. Nambyar, V.P. Raman, R. Krishnamoorthy and M. Subramaniam to the march of law

title: Chief Minister Joseph Vijay writes to Prime Minister Narendra Modi opposing proposed amendment to Food Security Act
content: In letter to PM, he urges the Centre to retain the existing entitlement of 35 kg of foodgrains per month for every household covered under the Antyodaya Anna Yojana, irrespective of family size

title: POCSO pendency and torturous process further victimise children subjected to sexual abuse
content: A staggering 18,733 POCSO cases are pending in various courts across the 38 districts in Tamil Nadu. The situation is exacerbated by patchy support services for children who opt to go through the procedural justice system

title: SIT probe reveals police conspiracy in Sai Krishna custodial death case
content: According to the investigating officers, the accused allegedly planned a conspiracy and attempted to create a story that the deceased had escaped from police custody and travelled to Goa, where his mobile phone signals were traced

title: Cosmoserve’s active debris removal tech to take flight with Skyroot’s Vikram
content: First demonstration of soft robotic capture in orbit using the technology that went from concept to flight-ready hardware stage in four months is to be undertaken

title: El Niño to dent India’s wind, hydropower output: study
content: The Centre for Research on Energy and Clean Air (CREA) projects that weaker wind and hydropower output, combined with rising demand for air conditioning, could open a generation gap of nearly 18 TWh (terawatt hour) over a period of one year to June 2027

title: Restore M.K. Stalin’s name in Nemmeli plaque, urges DMK
content: Former DMK Minister T.M. Anbarasan alleged the plaque had been covered with a cloth ahead of Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay’s visit to the plant for an inspection

title: Chief Minister Vijay visits desalination plants and drinks water
content: An official release said that Mr. Vijay directed officials to ensure proper maintenance of the Nemmeli desalination plants so that drinking water is supplied to the public without interruption

title: Congress targets Modi on foreign policy, cites passport decline and ‘silence’ on Gaza
content: Congress alleges that the government policies for passport have hurt India’s global reputation; also calls out PM’s silence on ‘Israel’s genocide in Gaza’

title: Chennai Corporation defers new infrastructure projects, cancels tenders worth more than ₹600 crore
content: An official says the tender cancellations have been implemented mainly to stagger expenditure and improve planning. The civic body has also not released the annual ward maintenance funds for civic repairs and sanitation, leaving many buildings, including UPHCs, in a damaged state

title: Mumbai rains | Rain severs Mumbai-Pune road link, three killed in landslide; Opposition slams govt.
content: The State government deployed a 30-member team of the National Disaster Response Force in Pune district, where a landslide occurred at Patan village in Maval taluka

title: NIA names LeT founder Hafiz Saeed as accused in Pahalgam terror attack
content: The charge sheet provides details of Pakistan’s conspiracy, Saeed’s role, and supporting evidence collected by the NIA in the case; LeT and TRF have been charged as legal entities for their “role in planning, facilitating, and executing the Pahalgam terror attack”

title: Jaishankar to launch campaign for India’s non-permanent 2028-29 UNSC seat as UN politics heats up
content: The bid will require substantial diplomatic mobilisation by India as Tajikistan will be the other candidate for the seat meant for the Asia-Pacific region

title: CBI searches 26 locations in Border Roads Organisation fund misappropriation case
content: Cases have been registered against 10 officers of the Lieutenant Colonel, Major, and Engineer ranks, besides private persons, the CBI says

title: Telangana gears up for second edition of global investors summit in December 2026
content: CM directs officials to further develop Taramati Baradari and Durgam Cheruvu converted into a full scale tourist attraction

title: Delhi HC gives two weeks to WFI to decide matter of show-cause notice to Vinesh Phogat
content: Phogat has been issued show-cause notice over accusations of indiscipline and anti-doping rule violations

title: Jalalabad in Shahjahanpur to be renamed ‘Parshurampuri’ by  U.P. government
content: Jalalabad is renowned as the ‘birthplace of Bhagwan Parshuram’; it finds prominent mention in mythological accounts and scriptures

title: 'Resigned judge' can't be impeached, say constitutional experts
content: Parliament will table an inquiry report on former judge Yashwant Varma's removal.  Justice Varma resigned in April after facing allegations of unaccounted money.  Constitutional experts state impeachment proceedings are now pointless after his resignation.  They emphasize a judge ceases to hold office upon resignation, not requiring acceptance.  The report's tabling is seen as a political formality with no effective purpose.

title: Govt got 'Satluj' pulled off OTT on security grounds
content: The government asked ZEE5 to remove the film 'Satluj' citing security concerns.  This action followed the movie's release on the OTT platform after a long delay.  The film depicts the story of activist Jaswant Singh Khalra and his disappearance.  Its makers released the movie on ZEE5 with a new name after censor objections.  Families of victims oppose the ban, believing the film acknowledges a painful history.

title: 'Operate autonomously': FIFA denies pressure as Trump confirms Balogun intervention
content: The clarification came after FIFA allowed Balogun to play in the United States' FIFA World Cup round-of-16 match against Belgium, a decision that drew criticism and raised questions over whether Trump's intervention had influenced the outcome.

title: Microsoft layoffs: Company cuts 4,800 jobs; to reduce 2.1% global workforce
content: Microsoft has laid off 4,800 employees, about 2.1% of its global workforce, as its new fiscal year begins, with Xbox and commercial sales taking the biggest hits. HR chief Amy Coleman told employees the business is changing because the industry around it is changing, insisting AI isn't replacing these roles. Xbox alone loses 1,600 jobs now, with 3,200 more planned this year as CEO Asha Sharma resets the gaming division.

title: Ram Temple ex-trustee Mishra failed to secure donations: SIT
content: A preliminary SIT report details alleged embezzlement of Ram temple donations. Senior Trust functionary Anil Mishra's oversight failure enabled suspects to pilfer funds. Nearly seventy instances of alleged pilferage were captured on CCTV footage. Subhash Srivastava, the counting room in charge, also faces serious lapses. The report names six suspects involved in theft and misappropriation offenses.

title: Bengal girl was gang-raped, brutalised, shows autopsy
content: A 12-year-old girl was sexually abused and brutally murdered in Baruipur. Her body was found in a local pond after she was dumped alive. The autopsy report indicated horrific injuries and suffocation as the cause of death. Police have charged three suspects under severe sections of the law. The Chief Minister vowed to seek capital punishment for all accused.

title: Weeks before Alexander Wang’s AI update tease, Google ‘messaged’ Meta
content: Google capped Meta's use of Gemini AI models around March 2026 after Meta sought more computing capacity than Google could provide. The restriction disrupted Meta's internal AI projects and forced employees to ration AI tokens. It came weeks before Meta AI chief Alexandr Wang announced a Muse Spark update aimed at cutting the company's dependence on rival models for coding and agentic tasks.

title: 'Huge cache of ammunition, war-like stores': Army recovers 5 AK rifles in J&K's Keran
content: Indian Army forces discovered a militant hideout in Kupwara's Keran sector.  They recovered five AK series rifles and one hand grenade during the operation.  A large cache of ammunition and other war-like stores was also found.  This significant recovery followed specific intelligence inputs received by the army.  The successful operation took place on Sunday, July 6, 2026.

title: 'Was alive when thrown into pond': Bengal girl's autopsy reveals bite marks across body
content: A horrific crime has emerged in West Bengal's Baruipur, where an 11-year-old girl, allegedly raped and murdered, was found to have been thrown into a pond alive. Preliminary post-mortem reports indicate severe injuries and water in her lungs, suggesting drowning as a cause of death following head trauma. Police are analyzing CCTV footage and have arrested a prime suspect, with investigations ongoing.

title: Jamie Dimon gives a 'reality check' to Canada's PM Mark Carney on his unity call to Europe
content: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon has dismissed Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney's call for middle powers to unite, calling it a "fantasy." Dimon pointed to Europe's economic struggles as an example of cooperation's limitations, citing high taxes and regulation. He emphasized that attractive business environments are crucial for growth, contrasting Europe's declining competitiveness with the U.S. market. Dimon also reassured investors about America's investment appeal, noting China's evolving global approach.

title: Non-state actors from outside India overwhelming Kuki areas in Manipur: Ex-UNLF chief
content: Former UNLF chairman Rajkumar Meghen is in Delhi seeking a coalition for peace. He urges economic integration between Kuki and Meitei communities to build trust. Meghen suggests external forces are involved in the ongoing violence in the state. He claims the government has failed to build confidence and stop the conflict. The violence has displaced thousands and claimed many lives since May 2023.

title: Declan Rice, Lauren Fryer's 2026 net worth revealed amid World Cup spotlight
content: Declan Rice, the English star player, has been a force to be reckoned with on the field in the last few years. While the Arsenal star did battle a few injuries in the last few matches, Declan Rice seems focused on making his nation proud through all the challenges. Even though his long term girlfriend and the mother of his son, Lauren Fryer, has been a constant support, she has largely stayed away from cameras.

title: Railway minister Vaishnaw launches new train services: Check routes and details
content: Railway Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw launched new train services and route extensions. These improvements enhance rail connectivity across Maharashtra, Uttar Pradesh, and Uttarakhand. New services connect Nanded to Mumbai and Tanakpur to Nanded. Existing routes were extended, and special services were regularized for passengers. Railways also announced special trains for Onam and Rath Yatra festivals.

title: Blake Lively, Ryan Reynolds reportedly devastated over Swift-Kelce wedding snub
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce just had the wedding of the year as billions across the world cheered them on throughout the world across countries. While the wedding pictures have been kept strictly private, Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds remained missing from the party. At a time when Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce publicly distanced themselves from the Hollywood power couple, it seems both Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds are furious at this.

title: Who is Erling Haaland's sister? Gabrielle goes viral at FIFA World Cup 2026
content: Erling Haaland has become one of the biggest stories of the FIFA World Cup 2026. While the Norway striker has impressed on the pitch, one of his family members has unexpectedly grabbed attention off it. His older sister, Gabrielle Braut Haaland, has gone viral after cameras spotted her cheering from the stands.

title: UK MP urges FIFA to delay Eng player's red-card suspension amid Balogun row
content: Quansah was sent off in the second half of England's round-of-16 match against Mexico at the Estadio Azteca after a sliding tackle on Jesus Gallardo. England were leading 2-1 at the time, with Jude Bellingham scoring both first-half goals.

title: Canadiens weigh Kirby Dach trade as roster shake-up looms before new season
content: The Montreal Canadiens are said to be considering trade options for forward Kirby Dach, as the organization continues its search for a reliable second-line center. There’s no deal on the horizon, but talks suggest the franchise is considering several roster options as it heads into the 2026-27 NHL season. Those conversations still revolve around Dach’s contract status, injury history and untapped potential.

title: Pak drone spotted over LoC in J&K’s Rajouri, search op launched
content: Security forces launched a search operation after a Pakistani drone was spotted near the LoC.  Blinking lights were observed over Mahadev Menka and Beripattan villages late Sunday.  The suspected unmanned aerial vehicle briefly entered Indian airspace before returning towards Pakistan.  Forces cordoned off the area and initiated a dawn search for any dropped payloads.  No suspicious items were found during the extensive search operation.

title: Samuel L Jackson’s quote on resilience: A lesson in confidence and perseverance
content: From modest beginnings, Samuel L. Jackson has evolved into a Hollywood heavyweight, symbolizing resilience and dedication. He attributes his ascent to the invaluable lessons from his grandparents, who implored him to embody exceptional qualities. By embracing this philosophy, Jackson has forged a path to become one of cinema's most admired talents, highlighting that unwavering commitment can break through the toughest odds.

title: NC to stage protest at Jantar Mantar for J&K statehood on July 20: Dy CM Surinder Choudhary
content: The National Conference will protest at Jantar Mantar on July 20.  Public rallies are scheduled in Srinagar and Jammu before the protest.  The party demands the restoration of Jammu and Kashmir's statehood.  This restoration was promised after the state Assembly polls concluded.  The absence of statehood impacts employment generation and development funding.

title: US county welcomed 37 data centres, now schools face 25% higher power bills
content: Henrico County, Virginia, is facing a nearly 25% surge in electricity costs, forcing public services to cut power usage. This comes as the county, a hub for data centers, grapples with rising energy demands and increased bills. While data centers bring tax revenue, they are straining the power grid, prompting calls for better management and reforms to balance supply and demand.

title: Who caught Taylor Swift's wedding bouquet? Meet the NFL insider behind buzz
content: Ashley Smith, sister of Chiefs player Trey Smith, caught Taylor Swift's wedding bouquet.  She attended the wedding as her brother's plus-one and secured the flowers.  This catch is considered significant as she works for the NFL in player engagement.  The event stayed within the Kansas City Chiefs family circle.  Fans are intrigued by this feel-good detail from the celebrity wedding.

title: Horoscope Today: Daily astrological predictions for July 7, 2026
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for July 7, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: Katherine Heigl to sell 25-acre Utah mountain home as family starts new chapter
content: Actress Katherine Heigl and musician Josh Kelley are selling their sprawling 25-acre Utah mountain estate for $10.6 million after nearly two decades. The custom-built home, a hub for their family life and creative pursuits, is being left behind as they embrace a new chapter in a smaller, nearby Victorian farmhouse.

title: J&K: Heavy rain triggers landslide near Kwar power project site, blocks Doda-Kishtwar highway
content: Heavy rainfall caused a major landslide near the Kwar hydroelectric project in Kishtwar. Flash floods and mudslides blocked the Kishtwar-Doda Highway, burying several vehicles under debris. Large volumes of mud and boulders entered the project site, damaging construction equipment and materials. Three gates of the Baglihar dam were opened as the Chenab river water level increased significantly. District administrations in Doda and Kishtwar are monitoring weather conditions and river flow.

title: Watch: 'Spiderman' takes charge on rain-hit Bhiwandi streets as civic chaos unfolds
content: A content creator dressed as Spiderman assisted stranded commuters on Bhiwandi's flooded streets. He directed traffic and helped pedestrians cross dangerous waterlogged stretches. Videos of his efforts gained widespread appreciation and drew significant social media attention. Momin also highlighted recurring civic issues and questioned administrative inaction on infrastructure. His actions sparked debate while preventing several minor accidents on the roads.

title: What Trump said to FIFA chief about Folarin Balogun's red card - Full conversation
content: US President Donald Trump confirmed his call to FIFA president Gianni Infantino.  He asked Infantino to review Folarin Balogun's red card and suspension.  FIFA later lifted the suspension, allowing Balogun to play.  This decision sparked praise from Trump and outrage from Belgium.  The suspension reversal was an extraordinary move in World Cup history.

title: Sundar Pichai recalls his father’s sacrifice to send him to America for higher studies
content: Sending children abroad for higher education involves significant parental sacrifice, as highlighted by Google CEO Sundar Pichai's father's year's salary for his flight. Beyond tuition, parents must plan for living costs, accommodation, and initial expenses. Encouraging children to pursue their passions, researching universities beyond rankings, and fostering open communication are crucial for a successful transition.

title: Bengal's Akanksha housing scheme: Affordable housing for state government employees
content: Bengal government's Akansha housing scheme offers state employees affordable flats on a lottery basis. Utilizing free government land, the project features various flat categories based on grade pay, with prices starting from Rs. 2200 per sq. ft. Serving government employees, excluding certain categories, can apply online with required documents and a Rs. 25,000 application fee. Property resale is restricted for 15 years or until retirement.

title: Cuba's national grid completely collapses, nearly 10 million in darkness
content: Cuba's national power grid collapsed, leaving ten million people without electricity. This nationwide blackout deepened the island's ongoing energy crisis. Ageing infrastructure and fuel shortages have caused frequent power outages for months. US sanctions are blamed by the government for limiting fuel imports. The situation has worsened amid a prolonged economic crisis on the island.

title: When MAGA trumped FIFA — Football caves to Trump tantrum, revokes US striker Balogun's suspension
content: Donald Trump contacted FIFA President Gianni Infantino regarding a red card suspension. FIFA then invoked an obscure rule to freeze the player's one-match ban. This action has caused significant controversy within the global football community. Critics argue that political interference should not influence sporting regulations. The incident highlights broader geopolitical and immigration themes connected to the player.

title: El Nino's heat test: India may face world's biggest energy system impact
content: India faces significant energy system impact from El Nino's arrival.  Renewable electricity generation may decrease while cooling demand rises.  This could create an electricity generation gap of nearly eighteen terawatt-hours.  Increased coal-fired power generation is the most likely outcome.  Expanding solar power and battery storage is critical for grid resilience.

title: How much did Khamenei's funeral cost? State spending draws scrutiny amid economic hardship
content: Iranians criticize the state funeral for Ali Khamenei due to extensive public spending.  This expenditure contrasts sharply with the economic hardships faced by ordinary citizens.  Critics allege authorities used state employees and free services to inflate funeral attendance figures.  Such actions highlight a perceived disconnect between the government and the general populace.  The funeral costs remain unconfirmed, with some estimates reaching $800 million.

title: When Haaland played against India: Norway star's FIFA WC heroics revive old memory
content: Haaland scored twice as Norway beat Brazil 2-1 to reach the World Cup quarterfinals for the first time. The striker headed in the go-ahead goal in the 79th minute before adding another before the end of regulation time to send his team through.

title: 'There can never be justice': Punjab 'fake' encounter victims' kin seek 'Satluj' release
content: Families impacted by enforced disappearances and staged encounters during the tumultuous years of Punjab’s militancy are passionately advocating for the screening of the controversial film 'Satluj'. They assert that the film sheds light on a vital yet suppressed segment of their history. Many recounted harrowing tales of loved ones taken by police and never seen again, with news of deaths often emerging from informal sources.

title: 145% rise in LPG imports: Gas buys to be doubled from US — how much can it help India?
content: Energy imports from the US have gained importance, particularly following the West Asia conflict, helping reduce the trade imbalance between the two countries. Besides the US, India has also sourced LPG from Argentina, Nigeria and Malaysia. Data from Kpler shows that India’s LPG imports from America have grown substantially since the US-Iran war began. After a marginal dip in April, the supplies have steadily climbed.

title: 'Made right decision': Trump confirms he asked FIFA to review Balogun decision
content: US President Donald Trump said he personally asked FIFA president Gianni Infantino to review the red card and one-match suspension handed to Team USA striker Folarin Balogun, a move that has sparked major controversy at the FIFA World Cup, according to CNN.

title: Allu Arjun virtual court appearance: Actor named accused in stampede case
content: Actor Allu Arjun virtually appeared before a Hyderabad court on July 6, 2026, regarding the fatal Sandhya Theatre stampede during a 'Pushpa: The Rule' screening. The incident tragically claimed the life of Revathi and injured her son. Arjun is named Accused No. 11, with theatre management as Accused 1-10. The court adjourned the case to July 29.

title: 'He is untainted in my eyes': Ram temple trust treasurer defends Champat Rai after resignation
content: Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust Treasurer Govind Dev Giri defended former general secretary Champat Rai following his resignation over a donation theft case, calling him "untainted" and his decision voluntary. The trust accepted resignations of Rai and trustee Anil Mishra after a preliminary report. Krishna Mohan takes over as interim general secretary, and a committee will recommend a permanent replacement.

title: Sanju Samson out of India squad; Ashok Sharma earns maiden call-up for Zimbabwe tour
content: Sanju Samson misses out on India's T20I squad for Zimbabwe due to a slump in form, having struggled in recent outings. Shreyas Iyer will lead the 15-member team, which includes a maiden call-up for pacer Ashok Sharma and retains young Vaibhav Sooryavanshi. Meanwhile, Shivam Dube has been added to the ODI squad as a replacement.

title: Trump's "Finish The Job" Warning To Iran Amid Peace Talks
content: Donald Trump on Monday said the United States would either reach a deal with Iran or "finish the job", while asserting that Washington has the capability to cripple Tehran's critical infrastructure if it chooses to do so.

title: Venezuela Earthquake: Death Count Rises To 3,535, Over 16,700 Injured
content: The death toll from Venezuela's twin earthquakes has risen to 3,535, with more than 16,700 injured in the disaster, according to updated official figures released on Monday.

title: Prince Harry Arrives In UK Amid Security Spat
content: Prince Harry arrived in Britain on Monday for a five-day visit expected to go ahead mostly without his wife and children after they were reportedly refused police protection.

title: Xi Jinping To Visit US In September, Says Trump
content: The President said the White House has struggled for decades to accommodate large state events, noting that the Executive Mansion currently lacks a dedicated ballroom.

title: Mourners Throng Funeral Procession In Tehran For Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
content: Khamenei's flag-draped coffin, and those of his family members who were killed Feb. 28 in an airstrike at the start of the war launched by Israel and the United States, sat aboard a truck decorated to resemble the ornamental grating that surrounds the shrine of an imam.

title: Hyderabad Woman Kills Husband By Injecting Toilet Cleaner, Arrested: Cops
content: A woman in Nizamabad district of Telangana allegedly killed her husband by injecting toilet cleaner through a cannula when he was undergoing medical treatment, police said on Monday.

title: 70 Applied For Citizenship In Assam Under New law, 6 Got It: Government
content: In yet another reply on the first day of the budget session, the Assam government said 1,72,673 foreigners have been detected in the state so far, while 31,786 have been deported.

title: Celina Jaitly To NDTV On Her Comeback After Years Of Harrowing Abuse And Grief
content: "My craft is what has stood by me through all my agonies and ecstasies of life. And I think this is my way of rescuing myself," Celina Jaitly told NDTV

title: 4 Dead As Pune Rains Trigger Landslides
content: Four persons, including three members of a family, were killed in a landslide and wall collapse, while two others were swept away in separate rain-related incidents as heavy showers battered Pune district on Monday.

title: "Tricks": Israel Demands Hamas To Disarm After Group Dissolves Key Gaza Body
content: Israel has insisted on the full implementation of the US-brokered peace plan under President Donald Trump.

title: Weather Highlights: Red Alert In Parts Of Maharashtra, Work From Home Advised Amid Heavy Rain
content: Monsoon Alert Highlights: The disruptions occurred amid the ongoing monsoon season, when periods of intense rainfall and gusty winds frequently impact air travel, particularly on high-density routes such as the Delhi-Mumbai corridor.

title: Man Strangles Wife During Argument, Dies By Suicide In Rajasthan: Cops
content: A 30-year-old man allegedly strangled his wife to death and later died by suicide in Rajasthan's Bhilwara district, police said on Monday.

title: Ram Temple Trust Interim General Secretary Krishna Mohan A Quiet Organiser
content: The resignation of Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust General Secretary Champat Rai, along with Trustee Anil Mishra, amidst a raging financial controversy.

title: Heavy Rain Claims Toddler's Life in Odisha, Flood Threat Looms Large
content: A two-year-old girl died and several others were affected as depression-induced rainfall lashed Odisha, with flood fears looming large in the Baitarani River, officials said on Monday.

title: Netanyahu Asks US Not To Sell F-35 Jets To Turkey Over "Power Balance"
content: US President Donald Trump travels later Monday to Ankara for a NATO summit, and his visit could be seen by the Turks as an opportunity to secure acquisition of dozens of jet engines and potential readmission to the F-35 fighter jet program.

title: Trump Rings Wall Street Opening Bell As He Ties Presidency To Market Gains
content: Trump rang the bell as a symbolic act that reflects how he has increasingly tied his presidency to the stock market.

title: Congress To Move High Court Over Meenakshi Natarajan's Rajya Sabha Nomination Rejection
content: Meenakshi Natarajan said a deliberate narrative was created to shield the Election Commission by suggesting that the nomination was rejected because of negligence within the Congress.

title: India Helps Unlock Universe's Secrets, From God Particle To Dark Matter
content: Buried deep underground, this extraordinary machine has already transformed humanity's understanding of the universe.

title: Siya Goyal Researched, Learnt From Sonam Raghuvanshi's Mistakes, Say Cops
content: While many parallels are being drawn between Siya Goyal and Sonam Raghuvanshi, the police say Siya Goyal researched online and was better prepared to execute the crime

title: BrahMos' Ex Top Official Traces Journey From Humble Start To Strategic Reach
content: The BrahMos of today is a vastly different machine from its initial coastal-defence avatar, which began with a highly specific mandate of creating a supersonic anti-ship missile

title: Govt proposes 3-year window for keeping vehicles in another state
content: road transport ministry proposes to extend to three years the period for which a vehicle can be kept in another state without re-registration . the move will complement the Bharat series registration, which allows eligible car owners to drive across all states and UTs .

title: Username feature row: Govt gives WhatsApp 3 more days to reply to notice
content: WhatsApp has given the govt three more days to respond to its notice over the proposed username feature . the feature could increase online fraud, phishing, impersonation and so-called "digital arrest" scams . initial deadline expired last week .

title: Keep Syama in, Singur out of textbooks: Suvendu Adhikari
